title: tk roadmap CLI: follow union edges in chain filter and annotate after edges
description:
Two gaps in cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap.go now that ComputeRoadmap layers on the union of hard+soft edges and RoadmapEpic carries After []string (json after,omitempty):

1. Chain filter: filterRoadmapChain (~line 51-68) computes the connected component via epic-to-epic blocked_by edges only (blockedByMap built from RoadmapEpic.BlockedBy). Soft-linked epics drop out of the scoped view — verified live: with e7c after-linked to o16, tk roadmap 4bt omitted e7c while bare tk roadmap layered it correctly in wave 3. Fix: build connectivity from the union of BlockedBy and After. Update the command Long help text (line ~22-23) which currently says "through epic-to-epic blocked_by edges".

2. Annotation: the per-epic line renders "← blocked by: id1 id2" for hard edges. Add a visually softer "← after: id1 id2" annotation for After entries, rendered AFTER the blocked-by annotation, using a dimmer/muted style than the blocked style (this is a preference edge, not a constraint). Follow the existing style conventions in the file.

Test cases (extend cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap_test.go, drive via ExecuteArgs only):
- Epics A, B with B after A: tk roadmap A output includes B; tk roadmap B includes A.
- Output for B contains "after: A" and does NOT contain "blocked by: A".
- Mixed: C blocked_by A and after B -> line shows both annotations, blocked-by first.
- JSON output unchanged in shape (after key already present from compute).

Files likely touched: cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap.go, cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap_test.go.
Run: gofmt -l cmd; go build ./...; go test -short -count=1 ./cmd/tk/...
acceptance_criteria: tk roadmap <epic-id> includes soft-linked epics in the chain; after edges render as a distinct annotation; go test ./cmd/tk/... passes
closed_reason: Completed: chain filter follows union edges (BFS both directions), blocked-by + muted after annotations added to CLI render (blocked-by was TUI-only — recon gap fixed), 4 new ExecuteArgs tests
